aoi.user_expression: Athabasca Basin margin, Saskatchewan, Canada; WallaStone Lake East
aoi.normalized_name: Wollaston Lake East / eastern Wollaston area
aoi.normalization_note: WallaStone is treated as a spelling variant of Wollaston. The focused screen uses the official eastern Wollaston electromagnetic map-sheet envelope rather than a claim boundary.
aoi.geometry_confidence: regional-envelope only; no authoritative polygon, claim id, or survey block supplied

deposit_model_assessed: Athabasca unconformity-related and shallow basement-hosted uranium

overall_screening_result.uranium_potential: moderate to high exploration potential at the area scale, driven by dense conductor evidence, radioactive boulder evidence, and favorable regional Athabasca/Wollaston context
overall_screening_result.confirmed_deposit_status: not confirmed inside the selected eastern Wollaston envelope by the queried uranium deposit footprint layer
overall_screening_result.confidence: moderate for regional target potential; low for drill-target ranking until AOI geometry, conductor quality, boulder source vectors, bedrock geology, alteration, and geochemical data are integrated

assumptions: WallaStone Lake East means Wollaston Lake East / eastern Wollaston area.
The focus envelope is based on the official NRCan/SGS eastern Wollaston electromagnetic map extents rather than a claim or property boundary.
User-reported subsurface conductivity is treated as a targeting hypothesis until source survey lines, depth slices, and conductor quality are reviewed.
EM conductors are treated as evidence for conductive bedrock or structure, not direct evidence of uranium mineralization.
data_gaps: No authoritative polygon, claim id, or target-center coordinate was supplied.
No live CDoGS analytical data were downloaded and normalized.
No assessment reports were retrieved from SMAD for the conductor files in this run.
No drillhole, alteration, radiometric, gravity, resistivity, or lithogeochemical data were compiled.
No glacial-transport analysis was completed for radioactive boulder source tracing.
No infrastructure distance calculation was performed.
recommended_next_steps: Define the AOI as a polygon or claim block and re-run all spatial queries in the authoritative Saskatchewan projected CRS.
Download and georeference NRCan/SGS Open File 8966 and 8967 sheets, prioritizing late-channel apparent conductivity, interpretation sheets, and magnetic derivatives.
Pull Saskatchewan assessment files 64E-0005, 64E-0015, 64E05-0035, 64E11-0029, 64E11-0027, 64E12-0043, and related records from SMAD.
Overlay conductor segments with radioactive boulders, uranium potential, bedrock domain, faults/structure, glacial drift thickness, and ice-flow indicators.
Use CDoGS surveys for background geochemical thresholds and compare only compatible sample media and analytical methods.

Rank target cells only where multiple evidence lanes coincide: conductor trend, structural complexity, radioactive boulders or geochemistry, favorable basement/cover setting, alteration, and drill support.
